channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Poking Around Athena Calderone’s Lavish New Home With Martha Stewart
item.description: The lifestyle influencer behind the brand EyeSwoon hosted a housewarming at her new home in TriBeCa.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Amir Hamja
item.media:description: A bartender, stationed in the shower, poured pricey champagne for guests.

item.title: Local Landscaper Tackles the Hamptons Bird Flu Problem
item.description: This winter, hundreds of dead geese fell around Georgica Pond. No one knew how to dispose of the infected bodies, so a local landscaper just winged it.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein

item.title: Want to Learn How to Strut Like a Model? Mandy Lieveld Can Help.
item.description: Mandy Lieveld teaches the leggiest people on earth how to use their limbs for fashion. Can she help an average woman like me?
item.dc:creator: Liana Satenstein and Eleanor Schmitt
item.media:description: The author learning to walk the walk in a 16th-floor rehearsal room in Midtown Manhattan.

item.title: The Duo Behind ‘A Twink and a Redhead’ Are Taking Their Comedy on Tour
item.description: Grant Gibbs and Ashley Gill have gained quite an online following with their outlandish comedy. Now they’re taking it on the road.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy and Jonah Rosenberg
item.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times

item.title: What Should I Wear to a College Graduation?
item.description: It can be tricky to look nice without stealing the spotlight. Our critic has some tips on dressing as smart as the graduate.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: How Louis Theroux Got ‘Inside the Manosphere’
item.description: In a new Netflix documentary, the filmmaker Louis Theroux exposes the motivations of men who populate a dark online ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Stefano Montali
item.media:credit: Alexander Coggin for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I am an interventionist, I’m a participant, I’m trying to drive the action,” Louis Theroux said of one method he deployed to investigate the manosphere.

item.title: Caro Claire Burke Wades Into the Tradwife Conversation With ‘Yesteryear’
item.description: “Yesteryear,” a debut novel about an influencer who is transported to the early 19th century, lands the author Caro Claire Burke in the middle of the culture wars.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Goodman
item.media:credit: Matt Eich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Caro Claire Burke, the author of “Yesteryear,” at her home in Charlottesville, Va.

item.title: What Were Bob Dylan and John Lennon Really Saying in the Back of That Limo?
item.description: A film scene of Dylan and Lennon at a low moment in 1966 has held a grim fascination for fans. Beneath the repartee, a touchy issue was at stake.
item.dc:creator: Jim Windolf

item.title: Half of Gen Z Uses AI, but Their Feelings Are Souring, Study Shows
item.description: A new study from Gallup found that young adults have grown less hopeful and more angry about artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann
item.media:credit: Andria Lo for The New York Times

item.title: Hasan Piker Can Stream, but Can He Stump?
item.description: The progressive and popular Twitch streamer, who is controversial with some Democrats, tests his political influence on the 2026 campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: Nic Antaya for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed took a selfie with Hasan Piker and the crowd at a rally in Michigan on Tuesday.

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Joy to Your Day
item.description: Indispensable emoji, undesirable friends and show notes for your life.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: My In-Laws Invited Us to Join Them on a Cruise. What’s an Introvert to Do?
item.description: Dreading the diminished opportunities for alone time during a weeklong cruise with her extended family, a reader looks for a graceful way out.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Modern Love: My Husband’s Breakdown Was My Breakthrough
item.description: Stefanie Gunning had to reach her lowest point to realize what she needed for herself.
item.dc:creator: Anna Martin, Reva Goldberg, Emily Lang, Davis Land, Amy Pearl, Sara Curtis, Elisa Gutierrez, Jen Poyant, Lynn Levy, Daniel Ramirez, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Pat McCusker

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Oh, to Be Loved Like That!’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: Nathan Farb, 85, Dies; Photographed Hippies, Siberians and Mountains
item.description: His camera captured subjects as diverse as New York City during the Summer of Love in 1967, Siberia under Soviet rule and the Adirondacks in upstate New York.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Linda Dresner, Old-School Guru of Avant-Garde Fashion, Dies at 88
item.description: Her minimalist gallery-like store on Park Avenue was a destination for generations of moneyed New Yorkers and helped change the way clothing is sold.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Exaggerated Silhouette
item.description: Shoulder pads and denim flaps made wardrobe basics look less ordinary.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Finasteride for Male Baldness is Rewriting the Rules of Male Beauty
item.description: A pill to cure baldness is changing the way men age — and how they see themselves.
item.dc:creator: Susan Dominus

item.title: Why Am I Watching People Get Their Medical Results?
item.description: What was once discussed with a doctor is now frequently encountered first as decontextualized data on a screen.
item.dc:creator: Peter C. Baker

item.title: “Cats: The Jellicle Ball” Live From the New York Times Office
item.description: Five of the show’s stars strutted and prowled amid the desks of T Magazine.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: Melania Trump’s Unexpected Easter Look
item.description: The first lady makes a statement in Ralph Lauren at the annual egg roll.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: The president and first lady mingled with young guests at the White House Easter Egg Roll on Monday.

item.title: Forget the A.I. Apocalypse. Memes Have Already Nuked Our Culture.
item.description: From our jokes and slang to the White House’s policy messaging, internet “brain rot” has escaped our phones to take over … well, everything.
item.dc:creator: Willy Staley

item.title: NYC Easter Parade and Bonnet Festival Brightens a Gray Sunday on Fifth Avenue: Photos
item.description: April showers brought silk flowers on Sunday as New Yorkers promenaded along Fifth Avenue in Manhattan for the annual Easter Parade and Bonnet Festival.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Some Single Women Are Celebrating Their Milestone Birthdays Like a Wedding
item.description: For some single women, milestone birthday parties offer the celebratory feel of a wedding, without the marriage.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

item.title: The New Wedding Prep: Fillers, Facials and Laser Treatments
item.description: For a growing number of brides, wedding planning also means months of aesthetic treatments, from injections to fillers and lasers.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Manners

item.title: ‘The Drama’ and the Secrets That Can Unravel a Relationship
item.description: In the new film, a shocking revelation upends the lives of a couple played by Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, raising the question: Are some betrayals simply unforgivable?
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

item.title: 50 Artists Travel Far and Wide to Learn From Filmmaker Werner Herzog
item.description: In January, 50 artists from all over the world were chosen to shoot shorts in the Azores, guided by the iconic filmmaker. They just had to find $10,000 and airfare to get there.
item.dc:creator: Saskia Solomon and Erin Schaff
item.media:description: Jordan McAfee-Hahn, left, shows Mr. Herzog and his longtime cinematographer, Peter Zeitlinger, footage from “Lola Dreams of the Sea,” a work in progress featuring a cow drawn to the ocean.

item.title: They Re-Planned Their Wedding in 10 Days
item.description: After cartel violence erupted in Mexico, just weeks before their wedding in Punta Mita, Lauren Futterman and Tyler Olson quickly decided to move the ceremony to California.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: May I End My Life With You?
item.description: My friend made the weightiest decision possible while staying at my home. I struggled with what it meant.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rehmeyer

item.title: First the Hora, Then the Hora Loca
item.description: Dani Roldan and Michael Wexler brought their families together, blending Jewish and Colombian traditions at their Miami wedding.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: He Likes the Mets, She the Yankees. That’s Where Their Differences End.
item.description: After connecting online, Lisa Tarantino and Billy Peeker bonded over their Long Island upbringings and their love of movies, breweries and baseball.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: After They Finally Met, Their ‘Rom-Com Story’ Began
item.description: Jacqueline Lobel and Michka Bengio started dating during the pandemic, though the two had briefly connected a few years before.
item.dc:creator: Alix Strauss

item.title: Is Alix Earle the Right Face for a Skin Care Brand? Reale Actives Has Its Skeptics.
item.description: Critics of Reale Actives, Ms. Earle’s new skin care line, suggest that the TikTok star’s past use of prescription acne treatments makes her an odd fit to sell consumer skin care products.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Alix Earle celebrated the release of her new skin care line this week.

item.title: Why Everyone Loves ‘Love on the Spectrum’
item.description: Without exploitation, “Love on the Spectrum” captures the triumphs and travails of dating. It has become one of Netflix’s most popular shows.
item.dc:creator: Anna Peele
item.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger for The New York Times
item.media:description: Madison Marilla and Tyler White, two stars of “Love on the Spectrum.”

item.title: The Revival of the Fashion-Tech Love Affair
item.description: Get ready for the return of wearables and a new stage in the fashion-tech relationship.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Adrienne Grunwald for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the new Meta Lab flagship on Fifth Avenue in Manhattan.

item.title: Bard College Is Split Over Its President and His Epstein Ties
item.description: Leon Botstein saved Bard from near ruin. Now, as an outside firm conducts a review, the campus is home to arguments about his legacy and future.
item.dc:creator: Ginia Bellafante
item.media:credit: Cole Wilson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A group of students and alumni opposed to the continuation of Leon Botstein’s presidency have formed a group called Take Back Bard.

item.title: A Hit Song Brought Hanumankind, Unexpected Fame. How Long Will it Last?
item.description: In 2024, Sooraj Cherukat, who performs as Hanumankind, released a single that climbed global charts. Now he’s aiming for more.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta and Kadar R. Small
item.media:description: Sooraj Cherukat, above, performs as Hanumankind, an East-West portmanteau of the Hindu god Hanuman and the word humankind.

item.title: What Drives Patrick Radden Keefe?
item.description: Patrick Radden Keefe’s carefully applied ambition has propelled him to a rarefied perch.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: London Falling: A Mysterious Death in a Gilded City and a Family's Search for Truth (Book)
Say Nothing: A True Story of Murder and Memory in Northern Ireland (Book)
item.media:credit: Erik Tanner for The New York Times
item.media:description: The writer at the home he shares with Gudzowska and their two sons.
